title: Rules
subtitle: Matching <1>is accent/case-insensitive</1>. "Whole word" prevents "paye" from matching "payweb".

dialog.title: Recategorize the entire history?
dialog.description: All enabled rules are re-applied to every transaction (excluding internal transfers). Your <1>manual choices</1> are preserved — only transactions with source "auto" or "default" are re-evaluated.

deleteRuleDialog.title: Delete the rule "{{keyword}}"?
deleteRuleDialog.description: The rule will no longer be applied to future imports. Transactions it already categorized stay as they are — re-run "Recategorize history" if you want to re-evaluate them.

groupedView.emptyState: No categories. Create one in the "Categories" tab.

normalizationHint.emptyWarning: ⚠️ At least one keyword becomes empty after normalization (pure prefix or date) — it will match nothing.

ruleCreateForm.keywordHelp: Separate with commas to create multiple rules at once.

categories.title: Categories
categories.subtitle: The <1>“kind”</1> feeds the sign guard rail: a category set to “Revenu” never applies to a negative amount. Sub-categories inherit their parent's type.

columns.name: Name
columns.internalTitle: Excludes the category from monthly averages (expenses/income). Useful for marking an internal movement — savings, transfer between accounts — without going through automatic detection.

table.dragDisabledTitle: This category has sub-categories — it cannot be nested.
table.confirmKindChange: Also change the type of the {{count}} sub-categories?

deleteDialog.title: Delete "{{name}}"?
deleteDialog.description: Rules pointing to this category will also be deleted (cascade). Transactions that were assigned to it will move to <1>no category</1> — you'll be able to find them again via the "Sort" tab.
deleteDialog.childrenWillBecomeRoots: Its {{count}} sub-categories will become root categories.
